title: Bitter Pickled Brazilian Cream Soup
description: A fragrant, monsoon-inspired soup that balances the tang of pickled mango with the richness of malai, perfect for a diabetic-friendly meal.

ingredients.name: Pickled green mango, chopped
ingredients.notes: Low-sugar pickled mango preserves a subtle bitterness

instructions: 1. Heat a large pot over medium heat and add a splash of water.
2. Add diced onion and minced garlic, sautéing until translucent and fragrant, about 3-4 minutes.
3. Stir in diced chicken breast and cook until lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
4. Add tomato paste and mix well to coat the chicken and onions, cooking for an additional 2 minutes.
5. Pour in 500 ml of water, bring to a gentle boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through.
6. Reduce heat to low and stir in 200 g of malai (coconut cream); let it warm through, about 3 minutes.
7. Transfer the soup to a blender or use an immersion blender to puree until smooth, then return to the pot.
8. Fold in 100 g of chopped pickled green mango, allowing the flavors to mingle for 2 minutes.
9. Season with 1 tsp salt and 1/2 tsp black pepper, adjusting to taste.
10. Serve hot, optionally garnished with a swirl of malai or fresh cilantro if desired.
